join_order.note: index lookup avoids full 60M lineitem scan; only ~5.88M rows accessed randomly

logical_plan.aggregation_note: o_orderdate and o_shippriority are functionally determined by l_orderkey via the join; key on l_orderkey alone with stored payload {orderdate, shippriority, revenue_sum}; ~3000 groups expected

phases.name: customer_scan_and_custkey_set_build
phases.description: Sequential scan of customer (1.5M × 5 bytes = 7.5 MB). Filter c_mktsegment == 1. Collect qualifying c_custkey into hash set.
phases.rationale: 7.5 MB fits in L3 cache (44 MB); parallel overhead exceeds benefit at this scale

phases.name: orders_scan_zonemap_filter_collect
phases.description: Parallel scan of orders (15M rows). Zone-map on o_orderdate skips blocks where zm.min >= 9204 (~50% block skip). Per surviving row: probe custkey_hashset AND check o_orderdate < 9204. Qualifying rows collect (o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority) into thread-local vectors. After parallel scan: merge thread-local vectors into global qualifying_orders flat_array and orderkey_meta compact_hashmap.

phases.name: lineitem_index_driven_aggregate
phases.description: Distribute qualifying_orders_array across 64 threads (each thread processes ~23K orders). Per qualifying order: lookup lineitem_orderkey_hash to get row-index range, iterate those lineitem rows, check l_shipdate > 9204, compute l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount), accumulate into thread-local agg_map keyed by l_orderkey. ~3000 groups per thread-local map fits in L1 cache (24 KB).

phases.name: merge_and_topk
phases.description: Sequential merge of 64 thread-local agg_maps into global agg_map (~3000 entries). Then single-pass top-10 heap scan over ~3000 entries with comparator (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC).

custkey_set.description: Open-addressing, power-of-2 capacity (2^19=524288 >= 301500/0.75=402000). ~2 MB. Probed during orders scan.

qualifying_orders_array.type: flat_array<struct{int32_t orderkey, int32_t orderdate, int32_t shippriority}>
qualifying_orders_array.description: Merged from 64 thread-local vectors after Phase 2. Used to drive parallel Phase 3 index lookups.

orderkey_meta_map.description: 2^21=2097152 >= 1470000/0.75=1960000. Maps qualifying o_orderkey to its (o_orderdate, o_shippriority) for aggregation result materialization. Probed in Phase 3.
orderkey_meta_map.note: Can be embedded directly in qualifying_orders_array if sorted and binary-searched, but hashmap is O(1)

thread_local_agg_maps.type: compact_hash_map<int32_t, struct{double revenue_sum, int32_t orderdate, int32_t shippriority}>[64]
thread_local_agg_maps.description: ~3000 groups, pre-sized to 8192 (next power of 2). 8192 entries × 20 bytes = ~160 KB per thread — fits in L2 cache. 64 threads × 160 KB = 10 MB total.

lineitem_orderkey_hash_index.description: Pre-built multi-value hash index. Zero build cost via mmap. Capacity=33554432. positions[60M] + HEntry32[33.5M]. Total ~642 MB.

topk_heap.description: Min-heap of size 10 with comparator: (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC). Single pass over ~3000 groups.

parallelism.phase3_sync: thread_local_agg_maps merged sequentially after barrier (trivial: ~3000 groups)

index_usage.layout: num_blocks=150, each block: {int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size}

index_usage.benefit: avoids full scan of 60M lineitem rows; accesses only ~5.88M rows for qualifying orders

memory_pattern.phase3: random_access_with_prefetch (index: 642 MB mmap; lineitem data: ~1.44 GB mmap, ~118 MB touched)

revenue_computation.accumulation: double addition per qualifying lineitem row into thread-local agg_map slot
revenue_computation.precision_note: Use Kahan summation if revenue precision is critical; standard double otherwise

estimated_cost.bottleneck: Phase 3 random access to lineitem data via index (~5.88M rows × 20B = ~118 MB random, parallelized across 64 threads)
